title: Citizen Science
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Citizen science involves ordinary people participating (0) in scientific research by collecting and analysing data. This collaborative approach has become increasingly popular as technology makes it easier for non-scientists to contribute meaningfully to research projects.||Citizen science projects cover (9) a wide range of topics, from astronomy and climate change to wildlife monitoring and medical research. Participants might count birds in their gardens, classify galaxies through online platforms, or track pollution levels in their neighbourhoods. The data collected by thousands (10) of volunteers can help scientists tackle research questions that would (11) be impossible to address alone due to the scale of observation required.||One of the most successful citizen science projects is eBird, (12) which allows birdwatchers worldwide to submit their observations to a central database. This has created an enormous dataset that scientists use (13) to track bird populations and migration patterns. Another project, Foldit, turns protein folding into a puzzle game, helping researchers understand diseases.||Citizen science benefits both researchers and participants. Scientists gain access (14) to data on scales they couldn't achieve otherwise, while citizens develop scientific literacy and feel connected to research. Critics worry about data quality, but studies have shown that (15) when properly designed, citizen science projects can produce reliable results. (16) As technology continues to advance, citizen science will likely play an increasingly important role in scientific discovery.

example.text: Citizen science involves ordinary people participating (0) scientific research.

questions.explanation: Expresión 'cover a wide range' indica gran variedad de temas.
Expresión 'thousands of volunteers' indica gran cantidad de personas.
Estructura condicional: 'would be impossible' expresa hipótesis.
Pronombre relativo que se refiere al proyecto eBird.
Infinitivo de propósito: 'use to track' indica el objetivo del uso.
Expresión 'gain access to' significa obtener acceso a algo.
Conjunción temporal que introduce la condición para resultados fiables.
Conjunción temporal 'As' significa 'a medida que' la tecnología avanza.
